reasons: Lolita reduces Granger's ranged skill-shot value by blocking incoming projectiles from the shield direction.
Granger's burst loses reliability when key shots are blocked or delayed.

proof.summary: Granger wants to land ranged projectile damage, while Lolita can block incoming ranged basic attacks and projectiles from the shield direction.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces Granger during his burst window.
Teammates stay behind the shield during Death Sonata pressure.
failureCases: Lolita is forced to shield another damage source.

reasons: Natalia can punish Granger before he sets up his ranged poke or ultimate angle.
Granger is vulnerable when assassins reach him from fog and force close-range decisions.

proof.summary: Granger wants safe ranged angles, while Natalia pressures him from concealment and forces him to respect flank vision.
worksBestWhen: Natalia tracks Granger's rotation before objectives.
Natalia attacks after Granger uses Rondo or stands away from peel.
failureCases: Natalia engages while Granger still has peel and repositioning.

reasons: Khufra can punish Granger's Rondo repositioning if Granger dashes across Bouncing Ball.
Khufra forces Granger to shoot from safer, less aggressive angles.

proof.summary: Granger uses Rondo to reposition around fights, while Khufra's Bouncing Ball punishes blink or dash attempts near him.
worksBestWhen: Khufra holds Bouncing Ball for Granger's reposition.
Khufra approaches from fog or objective terrain.
failureCases: Granger keeps distance and waits for Bouncing Ball to end.

reasons: Chou can close distance and kick Granger out of safe ranged positioning.
This is valuable because Granger is strongest when enemies cannot force him into close-range pickoff trades.

proof.summary: Granger wants to maintain ranged spacing, while Chou can use mobility, control-immunity timing, and displacement to isolate him.
worksBestWhen: Chou flanks rather than walking directly through Granger's poke.
Chou targets Granger after Rondo is used or forced.
failureCases: Chou engages through too much peel.

reasons: Saber can punish Granger when he is isolated or after he uses mobility.
Targeted burst limits Granger's ability to slowly control spacing with skill shots.

proof.summary: Granger wants space to aim and reposition, while Saber can force a targeted airborne burst window before Granger escapes.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits for Granger to use Rondo.
Saber attacks from fog or after frontline vision is broken.
failureCases: Granger stays protected by tank or support peel.
Saber dives before Granger's mobility is forced.
